observed.excerpt: FGAP-096 status: "identified", closed_by: null. Description current: "amendConfigEntryForDir loads the existing config (which validates it) before applying any mutation -- only a dryRun option, no force/skip -- so an already-invalid config cannot be repaired through the CLI: the write path refuses on the pre-existing invalid state before the corrective mutation runs. accept-all is not a recovery path either". Adversarial capability search: amend-config op exists (ops-registry.ts:1692) but is the validate-before-mutate path itself; only 'repair' hit in ops-registry is the reconcile op's derived-status repair (":2079 the repair half of the derived-status invariant class"), which repairs item statuses, not an invalid stored config. No config-repair/force path found.
observed.method: python3 json extraction of framework-gaps.json (FGAP-096); grep -rn repair packages/pi-context/src packages/pi-context-cli/src; sed -n 1692,1730p ops-registry.ts; grep 'name: "' ops-registry.ts | grep -i 'config|repair'

observed.excerpt: FGAP-065 status: "identified", closed_by: null. Description current: "A block_kind added to the catalog after a substrate was installed leaves that substrate with a dangling declaration: config declares the kind and its schema may be present, but its data file is never created, and no op materializes it. updateContext propagates schemas + config-registries only and contains zero block-data-file operations". Adversarial capability search: findUnmaterializedAssets (context.ts:762) is detection-only ("Pure read, no copy"); installContext materializes only install-time-declared assets; no update-path block-data-file materializer found (no seedBlock/materializeBlock function beyond the agent-tier materialization of TASK-119, which covers installed_agents, not catalog-added block kinds). No closing commit references FGAP-065.

observed.excerpt: Both divergence legs re-verified in current source. (1) Inline STRING: wf agent-spec.ts:125 verbatim `taskTemplate: task.template ?? task.inline,` -- a non-path inline string (resolvePromptField returns {inline: value}) is routed into taskTemplate, i.e. a template FILE name, while jit keeps the axes separate (`taskPrompt: taskField.inline,` / `taskPromptTemplate: resolveSpecPath(taskField.template,...)` at jit agent-spec.ts:258-259). (2) Object {inline}: BOTH resolvePromptField implementations handle objects only via `"template" in value` (wf :41-43, jit :26-29); `{ inline: ... }` falls through to `return {};` -- silently dropped by both -- and pi-workflows' own integration.test.ts:543 still authors exactly that dialect ('    inline: "Respond with a JSON object: ..."'). Filing absence: no filings/ dir; framework-gaps sweep for 'inline task' returns nothing; FGAP-129 (closed, TASK-104) covered the template-REF axis only. Static read: the wf compile THROW is the committed wave3 [re-run] tsx probe's result; the code shape it exercises is unchanged, but I did not re-run the compile probe.
